ranking_guard_rule: Keep v4 score unchanged; only allow ranking_eligible_D0 rows whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.50 on each signal_date.
weakness_reason: Liquidity guard improved relative performance and drawdown versus v4, but low-liquidity flag, TopK perturbation, and cost stress gates still failed, so the scheme remains non-promotable.

ranking_guard_rule: Keep v4 score unchanged; only allow ranking_eligible_D0 rows whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on each signal_date.
weakness_reason: Stronger liquidity guard further improved relative performance and low-liquidity alpha contribution share versus v6, but low-liquidity flag, TopK perturbation, and cost stress audit gates still failed.

universe_guard_rule: Keep v4 score unchanged; apply a liquidity universe guard at the pre-ranking universe boundary so only rows whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on each signal_date remain eligible for the effective universe.
weakness_reason: Moving the same 0.70 liquidity guard from ranking eligibility to the effective universe boundary produced effectively identical fixed-test results to v7 and still failed low-liquidity, TopK perturbation, and cost stress audit gates.
notes: Exploratory challenger that keeps the v4 momentum-only score fixed and changes exactly one core dimension: move the same 0.70 liquidity guard from ranking eligibility to the effective universe boundary. The completed run used signal_date_chunk_size=8 as an engineering-only experiment and remained a weak_candidate.

weight_mapping_rule: Keep the v7 score and 0.70 ranking liquidity guard unchanged; within each signal_date TopK, map base target_weight_D0 through one fixed linear liquidity penalty multiplier (0.5 + 0.5 * liquidity_rank) and renormalize the mapped target weights to sum to 1 before cohort scaling.
weakness_reason: Applying a single fixed liquidity-sensitive weight penalty on top of the frozen v7 selection contract slightly worsened annual_relative_return, relative_ir, drawdown, and low-liquidity alpha contribution share, while low-liquidity, TopK perturbation, and cost stress audit gates still failed.
notes: Exploratory challenger that freezes the v7 selection contract and changes exactly one core dimension: the mapping from selected TopK names to target weights. The completed run kept signal_date_chunk_size=5 and still classified the scheme as weak_candidate.

portfolio_extraction_rule: Freeze the recalibrated v7 score and 0.70 ranking liquidity guard. Keep the same selected Top10 names, but replace equal-weight extraction with one fixed rank-decay extraction: ranks 1-10 receive deterministic linear multipliers from 1.00 at rank 1 to 0.10 at rank 10, then are renormalized to sum to 1.0 before cohort scaling.
notes: Exploratory challenger that changes exactly one core dimension: the score-to-portfolio extraction geometry. The goal is to test whether a smoother top-slice extraction can harvest the weak momentum edge more robustly than the current equal-weight Top10 slice without changing score, guard, or execution semantics.
weakness_reason: Rank-decay extraction inside the frozen v7 Top10 worsened annual_relative_return, relative_ir, max_drawdown, and cost stress without fixing perturbation stability.

portfolio_refresh_rule: Freeze the recalibrated v7 score and 0.70 ranking liquidity guard. Replace full daily Top10 refresh with one fixed turnover-suppression rule: retain any currently held name that remains rank_position <= 20 on the next signal_date and still satisfies the frozen guard and tradability requirements; only refill vacated slots from the highest-ranked non-held eligible names until 10 names are held.
notes: Exploratory challenger that changes exactly one core dimension: the portfolio refresh rule across signal_dates. The purpose is to test whether preserving incumbents inside a fixed hold band can keep more of the weak momentum edge after costs without changing score, guard, TopK width, or execution semantics.
weakness_reason: Sticky top20 incumbent retention did not improve annual_relative_return, relative_ir, drawdown, turnover, perturbation stability, or cost stress robustness versus recalibrated v7.

ranking_guard_rule: Keep the confirmed VSUMD60 score unchanged; only allow ranking_eligible_D0 rows whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on each signal_date to enter the ranking universe.
notes: First restrained family seed promoted from the confirmatory VSUMD60 standalone winner. This seed changes exactly one core dimension: add a 0.70 ranking liquidity guard while keeping score, TopK, extraction, refresh, and execution semantics frozen.

ranking_guard_rule: Keep the confirmed VSUMD60 score unchanged; only allow ranking_eligible_D0 rows whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on each signal_date to enter the ranking universe.
weakness_reason: Guard70 reduced avg_turnover_daily and low_liquidity_weight_share versus standalone VSUMD60, but annual_relative_return, relative_ir, max_drawdown, and cost_stress_annual_relative_return all worsened beyond the prereg tolerance.
notes: First restrained family seed from VSUMD60 confirmatory winner. Rejected in seed round 1; do not treat ranking guard alone as the preferred transition path.

portfolio_extraction_rule: Linear rank decay across the frozen TopK head with same-signal-date renormalization.
notes: Second restrained family seed from the VSUMD60 confirmatory winner. This seed changes exactly one core dimension: portfolio extraction, while keeping score, guard, TopK, refresh, and execution semantics frozen.

portfolio_extraction_rule: Linear rank decay across the frozen TopK head with same-signal-date renormalization.
weakness_reason: The linear-rank-decay extraction kept turnover nearly flat and modestly reduced low-liquidity exposure, but annual_relative_return, relative_ir, max_drawdown, and cost_stress_annual_relative_return all remained slightly worse than the standalone VSUMD60 reference.
notes: Second restrained family seed from the VSUMD60 confirmatory winner. Rejected in seed round 2; extraction-only reshaping still did not justify replacing standalone VSUMD60.
